publication.showAs: Official Journal of the European Union, L 2024/1689, 12 July 2024
publication.name: oj

eventRef.showAs: European Commission proposal COM(2021)206 final adopted by the College of Commissioners
European Parliament first reading position adopted in plenary (Strasbourg) — P9_TA(2024)0138, 523 in favour, 46 against, 49 abstentions
Council of the EU formal adoption (unanimously)

Signed by the President of the European Parliament (Roberta Metsola) and the President of the Council — equivalent to promulgation in EU procedure (mapped to #promulgation for schema compatibility, see researchNote 8)
Published in the Official Journal of the European Union (L series, 12 July 2024)
Entry into force (20 days after publication in the OJ)
Chapters I and II become applicable (general provisions and prohibited AI practices) — 6 months after entry into force

Chapter III Section 4 (notified bodies), Chapter V (GPAI models), Chapter VII (governance), Chapter XII (penalties, except Art 101) and Article 78 (confidentiality) become applicable — 12 months after entry into force, per Art 113
General application of all remaining provisions — 24 months after entry into force
Article 6(1) becomes applicable — classification of AI systems as high-risk when used as safety components of products covered by Annex I harmonisation legislation; 36 months after entry into force

TLCOrganization.showAs: European Parliament and Council of the European Union (co-legislators)

TLCPerson.showAs: Roberta Metsola (President of the European Parliament, signatory)

proprietary.akndiff:actStatus: in force (staged application per Art 113: Chapters I-II from 2025-02-02; Chapter III Section 4 + V + VII + XII + Art 78 from 2025-08-02; general application 2026-08-02; Art 6(1) high-risk under Annex I product-safety legislation from 2027-08-02)
proprietary.akndiff:issuingBody: European Parliament and Council of the European Union (co-legislators in ordinary legislative procedure)

akndiff:amendsAct.showAs: Regulation (EU) No 167/2013 — agricultural and forestry vehicles
Regulation (EU) No 168/2013 — two- or three-wheel vehicles and quadricycles
Regulation (EU) 2018/858 — motor vehicles approval and market surveillance
Directive (EU) 2016/797 — interoperability of the rail system
Directive (EU) 2020/1828 — representative actions for collective consumer interests

proprietary.akndiff:researchNotes:
Friction log for EU / Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (AI Act) — Phase 1. Each note maps to a SPECIFIC schema field that strains under EU reality.
1. CO-LEGISLATOR AUTHORSHIP. ActTable.issuingBody is a free-text field. EU regulations are issued JOINTLY by the European Parliament AND the Council via the ordinary legislative procedure — neither alone is the author. We use the joint name "European Parliament and Council of the European Union", but that's a string, not a queryable join to two separate organisations. Same shape friction as ES (Cortes Generales = Congreso + Senado) but EU's case is more institutionally distinct because EP and Council are not in a hierarchy.
2. MODIFYING-LAW PATTERN, CROSS-COUNTRY CONFIRMED. The AI Act amends 9 prior acts. Spanish Ley 1/2026 amends 8. Schema's ActTable has no actSubtype field for self_contained vs modifying. Articles 102-110 of the AI Act each modify a different prior act, same structural pattern as ES articulos primero/segundo/tercero/ cuarto. Reproducible cross-country friction. Confirms ES finding §9.2 needs a v4 actSubtype column.
3. STAGED APPLICATION DATES. ActTable.effectiveAt is a single timestamp. AI Act has FOUR different application dates per chapter (Art 113): Cap I-II → 2025-02-02, Cap V/VII/XII → 2025-08-02, general → 2026-08-02, Art 6(1) high-risk → 2027-08-02. ES had ONE deferred provision (final 5.2). EU has FOUR. Cross-country confirmed; the deferred-applicability friction is qualitatively the same but quantitatively much worse in EU.
4. FOUR PARALLEL IDENTIFIERS. DocumentTable.nativeId is a single text column. The same EU act has CELEX (32024R1689), ELI URI (eli/reg/2024/1689/oj), OJ reference (L_2024/1689), and procedure file (2021/0106(COD)). ES has TWO (BOE-A-2026-7967 + ELI). EU has FOUR. We carry CELEX + procedureFile as akndiff: extensions but they are not indexed for queries — fetch-by-CELEX requires a body XML scan.
5. NEW OJ-L FORMAT (post-2023): ONE DOC PER ENTRY. JournalTable assumes one issue (issueNumber) contains many dispositions (BOE núm. 87 carries 50+ docs). Since 2023 the EU OJ uses per-act unique IDs (L_2024/1689) — each Regulation has its own OJ "issue" effectively. Our journal modeling becomes degenerate in EU (1 issue = 1 doc). Real schema friction for the journal/disposition relationship.
6. RECITALS: 180 OF THEM IN THE PREAMBLE. AKN's preamble accepts free prose, no dedicated <recital> element exists. Spanish Exposición de Motivos was 38 paragraphs (already noted as stretch). EU's 180 numbered recitals are 5x. We summarize them structurally rather than transcribe verbatim, matching ES style. Cross-country amplification of ES friction §9.4 (preamble limitation).
7. ANNEXES (13) ARE FIRST-CLASS LEGAL CONTENT. The schema doesn't extract annexes as queryable structures — they live as <hcontainer name="annex"> inside body XML. AI Act's Annex III (high-risk AI areas) is referenced 50+ times across articles and IS the operational rule that triggers Chapter III obligations. Annex XIII contains the criteria the Commission shall apply when designating GPAI with systemic risk per Art 51. Annexes here are NOT appendices — they are the substantive rules. A v4 schema should extract annexes to a side table for queries like "list all use cases under Annex III pt N".
8. NO "PROMULGATION" EVENT IN EU. The EU has no concept of promulgation in the national-legal sense. Acts become final upon SIGNATURE by the Presidents of both co-legislators (Parliament and Council) on the same date, then enter into force after publication in the OJ + 20 days. The loader's extractAct() REQUIRES eventRef[refersTo="#promulgation"] (loader.ts ~L567). To make the build pass we mapped the signature event to refersTo="#promulgation" — semantically equivalent for EU but strictly the EU vocabulary is "signature". Real cross-jurisdictional friction: the schema's vocabulary is borrowed from national-legal patterns (CL/ES) where promulgation is a distinct executive act after parliamentary approval.
9. EP/COUNCIL PARALLEL AMENDMENT TRACKS, then TRILOGUE. ES is sequential bicameralism (Congreso → Senado → return to Congreso). EU is PARALLEL: EP and Council both amend the Commission's proposal independently, then reconcile in trilogue, then both sign together. BillEventTable.chamber accepts a string but the sequence of events implicitly assumes chamber → second_chamber. For EU we use chamber="parliament" and chamber="council" on PARALLEL events with overlapping dates. The schema doesn't prevent this but doesn't model it either; queries like "what was the bill's state on date T" become ambiguous.
10. CROSS-INSTITUTIONAL PROCEDURE CODE (COD). ES expediente (121/000036) is per-chamber. The EU procedure file 2021/0106(COD) applies across Commission, EP, Council — it's the canonical cross-institutional reference. No dedicated schema field; carried in akndiff:procedureFile. Worth promoting to a real column if cross-country procedure tracking matters in v4.

longTitle.p: REGULATION (EU) 2024/1689 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 13 June 2024 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence and amending Regulations (EC) No 300/2008, (EU) No 167/2013, (EU) No 168/2013, (EU) 2018/858, (EU) 2018/1139 and (EU) 2019/2144 and Directives 2014/90/EU, (EU) 2016/797 and (EU) 2020/1828 (Artificial Intelligence Act). (Text with EEA relevance).

preamble.p: THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND THE COUNCIL OF THE EUROPEAN UNION,
Having regard to the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union, and in particular Articles 16 and 114 thereof,
Having regard to the proposal from the European Commission,
After transmission of the draft legislative act to the national parliaments,
Having regard to the opinion of the European Economic and Social Committee,
Having regard to the opinion of the European Central Bank,
Having regard to the opinion of the European Committee of the Regions,
Acting in accordance with the ordinary legislative procedure,
Whereas:

(1)–(8) Purpose, scope and policy objectives. The Regulation establishes a uniform legal framework for the development, placing on the market, putting into service and use of AI systems in the Union. Aims: trustworthy and human-centric AI, high level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights, free movement of AI-based goods and services, prevention of fragmentation across Member States. Legal bases: Articles 16 and 114 TFEU.

(9)–(20) AI definition and risk-based approach. Definition of AI system aligned with the OECD definition; distinguishes general-purpose AI models from AI systems built on them. Risk-based approach: prohibited practices, high-risk systems, transparency-only systems, minimal-risk systems.

(21)–(45) Prohibited AI practices (Article 5). Manipulative and exploitative techniques; social scoring by public authorities; predictive policing based solely on profiling; untargeted scraping of facial images; emotion recognition in workplace and education; biometric categorisation inferring sensitive attributes; real-time remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement (with narrowly defined exceptions).

(46)–(85) High-risk AI systems classification and requirements. Two routes to high-risk: AI as safety component of products under Annex I harmonisation legislation, OR AI listed in Annex III use cases. Requirements (Section 2 of Chapter III): risk management, data governance, technical documentation, record-keeping (logging), transparency to deployers, human oversight, accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity.

(86)–(105) Obligations of providers, deployers, importers, distributors and authorised representatives along the AI value chain. CE marking, EU declaration of conformity, registration in the EU database, fundamental rights impact assessment by certain deployers (Article 27).

(106)–(125) General-purpose AI models (Chapter V), added in trilogue 2023. Two tiers: GPAI models, and GPAI models with systemic risk (presumption: training compute > 10^25 FLOPs). Obligations: technical documentation, downstream provider information, copyright (TDM opt-out per Dir 2019/790 Art 4(3)), training-data summary. Systemic-risk additions: model evaluation, systemic-risk assessment, serious-incident reporting, cybersecurity. Codes of practice facilitated by the AI Office.

(126)–(140) Governance: AI Office (Commission DG-CONNECT), European Artificial Intelligence Board (Member-State representatives), Advisory Forum (stakeholders), Scientific Panel (independent experts).

(141)–(160) Market surveillance, post-market monitoring, serious incident reporting, EU database for high-risk systems (Article 71), conformity assessment procedures, notified bodies, harmonised standards and common specifications.
(161)–(170) Innovation support: AI regulatory sandboxes (Article 57), real-world testing (Article 60), measures for SMEs and start-ups, simplified obligations for microenterprises.

(171)–(180) Penalties (Article 99) — three tiers tied to category of breach; entry into force and staged application; relationship with the Charter of Fundamental Rights, GDPR, the Law Enforcement Directive, copyright law, product safety legislation, sectoral financial services and aviation/automotive type-approval frameworks. Final recital recalls subsidiarity and proportionality.
HAVE ADOPTED THIS REGULATION:

content.p: Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689 — 68 numbered definitions including 'AI system', 'risk', 'provider', 'deployer', 'general-purpose AI model', 'systemic risk', etc.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.

Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689 — eight prohibited practices including subliminal manipulation, exploitation of vulnerabilities, social scoring, predictive policing based solely on profiling, untargeted scraping of facial images, emotion recognition in workplace/education, biometric categorisation inferring sensitive attributes, and 'real-time' remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement (with narrowly defined exceptions in para 1(h)). Penalties for breach: up to EUR 35M or 7% of worldwide turnover (Art 99(3)).

section.heading: Classification of AI systems as high-risk

article.heading: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689. Two routes to high-risk: (a) AI as safety component of products under Annex I; (b) AI listed in Annex III use cases.
Estructura preservada. Commission empowered to amend Annex III by delegated act per Article 97.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689 — minimum elements per Annex IV.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.

article.heading: Transparency and provision of information to deployers

content.p: Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.
Estructura preservada. Texto íntegro en CELEX:32024R1689.

section.heading: Obligations of providers and deployers of high-risk AI systems and other parties

article.heading: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada. 12 obligations enumerated.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada — 10-year retention.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Corrective actions and duty of information

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Authorised representatives of providers of high-risk AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada — required for third-country providers.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Responsibilities along the AI value chain

content.p: Estructura preservada — substantial-modification clause triggers re-classification as provider.

article.heading: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada — 12 paragraphs incl. ex-ante judicial authorisation for post-remote biometric identification.

article.heading: Fundamental rights impact assessment for high-risk AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada — FRIA produced by deployer (public bodies, private entities providing public services, certain Annex III deployers) before first use.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Application of a conformity assessment body for notification

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Presumption of conformity with requirements relating to notified bodies

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Subsidiaries of notified bodies and subcontracting

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Identification numbers and lists of notified bodies

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Challenge to the competence of notified bodies

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Conformity assessment bodies of third countries

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Presumption of conformity with certain requirements

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada — internal control (Annex VI) or notified-body assessment (Annex VII).
Estructura preservada — 4-5 year validity.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada — 15-day standstill for objections.
Estructura preservada — content per Annex V.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada — registration in EU database (Article 71).

chapter.heading: TRANSPARENCY OBLIGATIONS FOR PROVIDERS AND DEPLOYERS OF CERTAIN AI SYSTEMS

article.heading: Transparency obligations for providers and deployers of certain AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada. AI watermarking, deepfake disclosure, emotion-recognition / biometric-categorisation deployer disclosure.

article.heading: Classification of general-purpose AI models as general-purpose AI models with systemic risk

content.p: Estructura preservada. Presumption: training compute > 10^25 FLOPs.
Estructura preservada.

section.heading: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models

article.heading: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models

content.p: Estructura preservada — technical documentation (Annex XI), downstream-provider information (Annex XII), copyright TDM opt-out, training-data summary.

article.heading: Authorised representatives of providers of general-purpose AI models

content.p: Estructura preservada.

section.heading: Obligations of providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk

article.heading: Obligations of providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk

content.p: Estructura preservada — model evaluation, systemic-risk mitigation, serious-incident reporting, cybersecurity.
Estructura preservada — facilitated by AI Office.
Estructura preservada — at least one national sandbox per Member State by 2 August 2026.

article.heading: Detailed arrangements for, and functioning of, AI regulatory sandboxes

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Further processing of personal data for developing certain AI systems in the public interest in the AI regulatory sandbox

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Testing of high-risk AI systems in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Informed consent to participate in testing in real world conditions outside AI regulatory sandboxes

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Measures for providers and deployers, in particular SMEs, including start-ups

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada — simplified QMS for microenterprises.
Estructura preservada — Commission DG-CONNECT.

article.heading: Establishment and structure of the European Artificial Intelligence Board

content.p: Estructura preservada — one rep per Member State + EDPS observer.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada — stakeholders, FRA, ENISA, CEN, CENELEC, ETSI permanent.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Access to the pool of experts by the Member States

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Designation of national competent authorities and single points of contact

content.p: Estructura preservada — by 2 August 2025.

chapter.heading: EU DATABASE FOR HIGH-RISK AI SYSTEMS LISTED IN ANNEX III

article.heading: EU database for high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III

content.p: Estructura preservada — public except secure non-public sections for law enforcement / migration / asylum / border control.

chapter.heading: POST-MARKET MONITORING, INFORMATION SHARING AND MARKET SURVEILLANCE

article.heading: Post-market monitoring by providers and post-market monitoring plan for high-risk AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada — Commission template by 2 February 2026.

section.heading: Sharing of information on serious incidents

content.p: Estructura preservada — 15-day default; 2-day for widespread infringement / critical infrastructure malfunction; 10-day for death.

article.heading: Market surveillance and control of AI systems in the Union market

content.p: Estructura preservada — Reg (EU) 2019/1020 applies; EDPS for Union institutions.

article.heading: Mutual assistance, market surveillance and control of general-purpose AI systems

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Supervision of testing in real world conditions by market surveillance authorities

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Powers of authorities protecting fundamental rights

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Procedure at national level for dealing with AI systems presenting a risk

content.p: Estructura preservada — 3-month standstill for Commission decision.

article.heading: Procedure for dealing with AI systems classified by the provider as non-high-risk in application of Annex III

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Compliant AI systems which present a risk

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Right to lodge a complaint with a market surveillance authority

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Right to explanation of individual decision-making

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Reporting of infringements and protection of reporting persons

content.p: Estructura preservada — Whistleblower Directive (EU) 2019/1937 applies.

section.heading: Supervision, investigation, enforcement and monitoring in respect of providers of general-purpose AI models

article.heading: Enforcement of the obligations of providers of general-purpose AI models

content.p: Estructura preservada — exclusive Commission/AI Office competence.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Alerts of systemic risks by the scientific panel

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Power to request documentation and information

content.p: Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.
Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Procedural rights of economic operators of the general-purpose AI model

content.p: Estructura preservada — Reg 2019/1020 Art 18 mutatis mutandis.

article.heading: Codes of conduct for voluntary application of specific requirements

content.p: Estructura preservada.

article.heading: Guidelines from the Commission on the implementation of this Regulation

content.p: Estructura preservada.

chapter.heading: DELEGATION OF POWER AND COMMITTEE PROCEDURE

content.p: Estructura preservada — 5-year delegation from 1 August 2024, tacitly extended.
Estructura preservada — Reg (EU) 182/2011 examination procedure.

Estructura preservada — three-tier administrative fines: up to EUR 35M or 7% worldwide turnover for Art 5 prohibited practices; up to EUR 15M or 3% for other operator/notified-body breaches; up to EUR 7.5M or 1% for incorrect information. SME-friendly cap: lower of percentages or amount.

article.heading: Administrative fines on Union institutions, bodies, offices and agencies

content.p: Estructura preservada — EDPS imposes; up to EUR 1.5M for Art 5 breaches, EUR 750K for others.

article.heading: Fines for providers of general-purpose AI models

content.p: Estructura preservada — up to 3% worldwide turnover or EUR 15M, imposed by Commission.
Adds subparagraph to Reg (EC) 300/2008 Art 4(3) requiring Chapter III Section 2 of this Regulation be taken into account when adopting AI safety equipment specifications.
Adds subparagraph to Reg (EU) 167/2013 Art 17(5) on AI safety components in agricultural/forestry vehicles.
Adds subparagraph to Reg (EU) 168/2013 Art 22(5) on AI safety components in two/three-wheel vehicles.
Adds new paragraph to Directive 2014/90/EU Art 8 on AI safety components in marine equipment.
Adds new paragraph to Directive (EU) 2016/797 Art 5 on AI safety components in rail interoperability.
Adds new paragraph to Reg (EU) 2018/858 Art 5 on AI safety components in motor-vehicle approval.
Six paragraphs added to Reg (EU) 2018/1139 Arts 17, 19, 43, 47, 57, 58 — most-modified target act.
Adds paragraph to Reg (EU) 2019/2144 Art 11 on AI safety components in motor-vehicle general safety. This is the only target act fully MODELED in this corpus.
Adds point (68) to Annex I of Directive (EU) 2020/1828 — adds AI Act to the list of acts protected by the representative-action mechanism.

article.heading: AI systems already placed on the market or put into service and general-purpose AI models already placed on the market

content.p: Estructura preservada — transitional provisions for pre-existing AI systems and GPAI models.
Estructura preservada — annual review of Annex III and Art 5; quadrennial Commission evaluation by 2 August 2028.

Entry into force: 20 days after OJ publication (1 August 2024). General application: 2 August 2026. Chapters I and II from 2 February 2025. Chapter III Section 4, Chapter V, Chapter VII, Chapter XII (except Art 101) and Article 78 from 2 August 2025. Article 6(1) from 2 August 2027.

Estructura preservada. Section A (NLF-based, 12 acts: Machinery 2006/42/EC, Toys 2009/48/EC, Recreational craft, Lifts, ATEX, Radio equipment, Pressure equipment, Cableways, PPE, Gas appliances, MDR 2017/745, IVDR 2017/746). Section B (other, 8 acts: Civil aviation security 300/2008, Agricultural vehicles 167/2013, Two/three-wheel 168/2013, Marine equipment 2014/90, Rail 2016/797, Motor vehicles 2018/858, Civil aviation 2018/1139, General safety motor vehicles 2019/2144).

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: List of criminal offences referred to in Article 5(1)(h)(iii)

content.p: Estructura preservada. Lists criminal offences for which 'real-time' remote biometric identification in publicly accessible spaces by law enforcement is exceptionally permitted, based on Council Framework Decision 2002/584/JHA: terrorism, trafficking, sexual exploitation of children, drugs, arms, murder, organ trafficking, kidnapping, ICC crimes, aircraft/ship seizure, rape, environmental crime, organised robbery, sabotage, criminal organisation participation.

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

content.p: Estructura preservada. Annex III is the OPERATIONAL list that drives Chapter III obligations — referenced 50+ times in articles. Eight areas: 1. Biometrics (remote identification, categorisation, emotion recognition); 2. Critical infrastructure (digital, road traffic, water/gas/heat/electricity); 3. Education and vocational training; 4. Employment and workers' management; 5. Access to essential private and public services (incl. credit scoring, insurance pricing, emergency dispatch); 6. Law enforcement (victim risk, polygraph, evidence reliability, recidivism risk, profiling); 7. Migration, asylum and border control management; 8. Administration of justice and democratic processes (judicial assistance, election influence).

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Technical documentation referred to in Article 11(1)

content.p: Estructura preservada. Nine elements of technical documentation provider must produce: general description, detailed description (development methods/architecture/data/training/oversight/cybersecurity), monitoring/control description, performance metrics, risk management system description, change history, harmonised standards applied, EU declaration of conformity copy, post-market monitoring system description.
Estructura preservada. Mandatory content of the EU declaration of conformity (Art 47).

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Conformity assessment procedure based on internal control

content.p: Estructura preservada. Procedure used for Annex III pts 2-8 high-risk systems and for Annex III pt 1 systems where harmonised standards are followed.

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Conformity based on an assessment of the quality management system and an assessment of the technical documentation

content.p: Estructura preservada. Notified-body conformity assessment procedure required for Annex III pt 1 systems where harmonised standards are not fully applied.

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Information to be submitted upon the registration of high-risk AI systems in accordance with Article 49

content.p: Estructura preservada. Sections A (provider registration per Art 49(1)), B (provider registration per Art 49(2) — non-high-risk Annex III), C (deployer registration per Art 49(3) — public bodies).

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Information to be submitted upon the registration of high-risk AI systems listed in Annex III in relation to testing in real world conditions in accordance with Article 60

content.p: Estructura preservada.

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Union legislative acts on large-scale IT systems in the area of Freedom, Security and Justice

content.p: Estructura preservada. Lists EU large-scale IT systems with embedded AI: SIS, VIS, Eurodac, ETIAS, EES, ECRIS-TCN — these are subject to extended transitional period (compliance by 31 December 2030 per Art 111(1)).

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Technical documentation referred to in Article 53(1)(a) — technical documentation for providers of general-purpose AI models

content.p: Estructura preservada.

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Transparency information referred to in Article 53(1)(b) — for downstream providers integrating GPAI models

content.p: Estructura preservada.

hcontainer.name: annex
hcontainer.heading: Criteria for the designation of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk referred to in Article 51

content.p: Estructura preservada. Seven criteria the Commission shall apply: (a) parameter count, (b) data set size/quality (token count), (c) compute (FLOPs/training time), (d) input/output modalities, (e) capability benchmarks, (f) Union end-user reach, (g) Union business-user reach. This annex IS the substantive rule for Art 51(b) systemic-risk designation, not an appendix.

conclusions.p: This Regulation shall be binding in its entirety and directly applicable in all Member States.
